excerpt:
Rocky Mountain Area(PL 4)   
Fire Activity and Teams Assigned Totals 
New fires: 11 
New large incidents: 1 
Uncontained large fires: 6 
CIMTs Committed: 4 
 
Aspen Acres, Custer County. CIMT (AK Team 1). Twenty miles southwest of Pueblo, CO. Timber and grass. 
Active fire behavior with uphill runs, running and torching. Numerous residences and communication 
infrastructure threatened. Area, road and trail closures in effect. Precipitation occurred over the fire area 
yesterday. 
 
Gold Mountain, Grand Mesa, Uncompahgre and Gunnison NF, USFS. CIMT (RM Team 3). Two miles north 
of Ouray, CO. Grass, brush and closed timber litter. Active fire behavior with uphill runs and group torching. 
Numerous structures and critical infrastructure threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
Willow, Pike and San Isabel NF, USFS. CIMT (SW Team 4). Six miles west of Leadville, CO. Brush, closed 
timber litter and timber. Active fire behavior with short crown runs, wind-driven runs and long-range spotting. 
Numerous structures and critical infrastructure threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
Ferris, San Juan NF, USFS. CIMT (CA Team 7) mobilizing. Seven miles east of Cahone, CO. Timber and 
brush. Extreme fire behavior with crowning, running and torching. Numerous structures and energy 
infrastructure threatened. Evacuations, area, road and trail closures in effect. 
 
* Twin Creek, Wind River/Bighorn Basin District, BLM. Sixteen miles southeast of Lander, WY. Brush, timber 
and grass. Active fire behavior. 
 
West Bridger Creek, Hotsprings County. Nineteen miles southeast of Thermopolis, WY. Grass and brush. No 
new information.
